title: Ordered CatBoost on raw categoricals instead of frequency-only encoding
summary: The dataset has very high-cardinality Driver and Race interactions, and CatBoost’s ordered statistics were designed exactly for this leakage-prone setting.
rationale: The dataset has very high-cardinality Driver and Race interactions, and CatBoost’s ordered statistics were designed exactly for this leakage-prone setting. The current best solutions mostly reduce categoricals to frequency features plus manual crosses, leaving target-conditional structure underused.
implementation_hint: Train CatBoostClassifier directly on raw categorical columns and a small set of numeric features, preserving a chronological order such as Year -> Race -> LapNumber within driver sequence. Test raw categories plus crosses like Driver×Compound, Race×Compound, and Race×Stint, then blend CatBoost logits with the existing LightGBM/XGBoost-style model.
expected_effect: Should recover informative historical priors for rare drivers/races and interact them with compound/stint without hand-built smoothing logic.
risk: If folds are not grouped by race chronology, ordered target statistics can still look better locally than they generalize; use grouped OOF by Year-Race and compare to the current frequency baseline.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
